phase_noise_prior.derivation: Median from 3.1% average phase stability times 2pi; 95th anchor from 35 degree maximum flicker.

wavelength_prior.status: not numerically fixed from panel fabrication alone
wavelength_prior.note: Panel calibration must be wavelength-specific; close calibrated wavelengths can remain similar, but the relevant quantitative prior belongs to the source and calibration stack rather than the fabricated panel itself.

source_refs.title: Fast measurement of the phase flicker of a digitally addressable LCoS-SLM
source_refs.note: Average phase stability about 3.1%; maximal phase flicker about 30 degrees for a HOLOEYE Pluto device at 633 nm.

source_refs.title: LCoS SLM Study and Its Application in Wavelength Selective Switch
source_refs.note: GAEA device with 3.74 um pitch shows maximum flicker about 35 degrees; cooling to -8 C can reduce flicker by up to 80%.

source_refs.title: Phase Compensation of the Non-Uniformity of the Liquid Crystal on Silicon Spatial Light Modulator at Pixel Level
source_refs.note: HOLOEYE device with 8 um pitch and 87% fill factor; phase response must be recalibrated across wavelength and temperature.

source_refs.title: Liquid Crystal Spatial Light Modulator with Optimized Phase Modulation Ranges to Display Multiorder Diffractive Elements
source_refs.note: Phase response does not vary remarkably for a different but close illuminating wavelength after calibration.

phase_noise_prior.physical_origin: 10 nm diameter variation mapped to about pi/4 dephasing.

lateral_misalignment_prior.physical_origin: 50 nm bilayer misalignment tolerance; 100 nm and 250 nm explicitly studied as stronger displacement cases.

rotation_prior.status: requires device-specific overlay metrology for exact degree-scale calibration
rotation_prior.note: Current simulation rotation term should be interpreted as a companion variable to bilayer overlay error rather than as an independently measured literature distribution.

wavelength_prior.physical_origin: 10 nm geometry error induces about 25 nm shift of the wavelength of maximum diffraction efficiency at 750 nm.

source_refs.title: Challenges in nanofabrication for efficient optical metasurfaces
source_refs.note: 10 nm diameter error yields dephasing close to pi/4 and about 25 nm peak-wavelength shift at 750 nm; high-performance devices require better than 10 nm precision.

source_refs.title: Free-standing bilayer metasurfaces in the visible
source_refs.note: Bilayer structures show robust performance under misalignment tolerance within 50 nm; unit-cell size is 420 nm.

source_refs.title: Bilayer optical metasurfaces with multiple broken symmetries for nonlinear wavelength generation
source_refs.note: Controlled horizontal-displacement studies explicitly evaluate 100 nm and 250 nm layer offsets in a bilayer metasurface.
